lede: A look at January 8 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Zach Charbonnet and Cynthia Erivo, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Jair Bolsonaro
events.desc: Supporters of former Brazil president Jair Bolsonaro storm the Brazilian Congress.

events.title: Front for the Liberation of the Enclave of Cabinda
events.desc: Gunmen from an offshoot of the Front for the Liberation of the Enclave of Cabinda attack a bus carrying the Togo national football team on its way to the 2010 Africa Cup of Nations, killing three people and injuring another nine.

events.title: Antonov An-32
events.desc: An Antonov An-32 cargo aircraft crashes into a crowded market in Kinshasa, Zaire, killing up to 223 people on the ground; two of six crew members are also killed.

events.title: 1977 Moscow bombings
events.desc: Three bombs explode in Moscow, Russia, Soviet Union, within 37 minutes, killing seven. The bombings are attributed to an Armenian separatist group.

events.title: Andrei Zhdanov
events.desc: Andrei Zhdanov, Chairman of the Finnish Allied Commission, submits to the Finnish War Criminal Court an interrogation report by General Erich Buschenhagen, a German prisoner of war, on the contacts between Finnish and German military personnel before the Continuation War and a copy of Hitler's Barbarossa plan.

events.title: Woodrow Wilson
events.desc: U.S. President Woodrow Wilson announces his "Fourteen Points" as conditions for ending World War I.

events.title: Herman Hollerith
events.desc: Herman Hollerith is issued US patent #395,791 for the 'Art of Applying Statistics' — his punched card calculator.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Second Battle of Springfield.

events.title: George Washington
events.desc: George Washington delivers the first State of the Union address in New York City.

events.title: George Frideric Handel
events.desc: The premiere of George Frideric Handel's Ariodante takes place at the Royal Opera House, Covent Garden.

births.name: R. Kelly
births.line: American singer-songwriter, producer, and sex offender

deaths.text: Alexander Prokhorov, Australian-Russian physicist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate
